description: Base paragraph direction: dir="rtl" and CSS direction: rtl set the UAX #9 base level (RTL runs reorder, base level flips), right-align by default, and inherit to descendant blocks; an explicit text-align overrides the direction default. Hebrew/Arabic glyphs come from the fallback chain (Arial Unicode MS), so PDF text is embedded (not literal in the stream).
visual_assertions: The LTR paragraph is left-aligned; the RTL paragraphs are right-aligned.
Hebrew and Arabic paragraphs read right-to-left with correct word order.
The mixed paragraph places the embedded English word in its own left-to-right order between the Hebrew words.

A

inside

is right-aligned by inheritance; a sibling with text-align: left is left-aligned but still reads right-to-left.
